subject: Public Health
title: Why Pandemics Repeat
description: A 60-second lesson on Why Pandemics Repeat.

hook.text: Humans forget pandemics fast. The 1918 flu killed millions but barely appears in history textbooks.

buildup.text: After each crisis, funding surges—then quietly evaporates once the headlines fade.

discovery.text: Deforestation, urbanization, and factory farming keep creating new opportunities for viruses to jump species.

twist.text: We've known the next pandemic was coming for decades. Reports warned. Governments filed them away.

climax.text: The gap between knowing and acting is the most dangerous vulnerability in public health.

punchline.text: The lesson is always the same. The forgetting is, too.

quiz.question: Why do pandemics keep catching the world off guard?
options: Funding and attention fade after each crisis ends
